name: Genil River Valley Loop from Corcoya
name_original: Valle del Río Genil — circular desde Corcoya
description: A difficult 9.8-mile (15.8 km) jogging route in the Valle del Río Genil, offering river views and a challenging 1061-foot (323 m) climb.
region_intro: Jogging around Badolatosa offers a diverse landscape primarily shaped by the Genil River, providing numerous riverside paths. The region features a mix of urban sections and natural river meanders, with some routes extending into surrounding valleys. Terrain varies from relatively flat, easy jogging routes along the riverbanks to more challenging paths with moderate elevation gains. Best jogging routes around Badolatosa The most popular jogging route is Puente Genil - run through the urban area and the Genil river, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail that takes about 28 minutes to complete. This easy route combines urban exploration with scenic river views. Another top favourite among local runners is Genil River Meander Trail, an easy 2.7 miles (4.4 km) path. This route follows the natural curves of the Genil River, offering a tranquil running experience. Local runners also love the Genil River Meander and Fuensanta Hermitage Trail, a 8.7 miles (13.9 km) trail leading through river meanders and past historical sites, often completed in about 1 hour 42 minutes. Jogging around Badolatosa is defined by river valleys, urban sections, and historical landmarks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside paths to more moderate trails with varied terrain. The routes in Badolatosa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 150 runners have used komoot to explore Badolatosa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Badolatosa?
A: There are over 25 running routes around Badolatosa, offering a variety of options for different preferences and fitness levels. The region is known for its diverse landscape, primarily shaped by the Genil River.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails around Badolatosa?
A: You can expect a mix of terrain. Many routes feature relatively flat paths along the Genil River, while others extend into surrounding valleys with moderate elevation gains. Some trails combine urban sections with natural river meanders.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners in Badolatosa?
A: Yes, Badolatosa offers several easy running routes perfect for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001013710/puente-genil-run-through-the-urban-area-and-the-genil-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Genil - run through the urban area and the Genil river&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.7-mile trail that combines urban exploration with scenic river views. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1287245449/genil-river-meander-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Genil River Meander Trail&lt;/a&gt;, also an easy 2.7 miles, offering a tranquil experience along the river.

Q: Are there more challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a greater challenge, Badolatosa has routes with more significant elevation changes and longer distances. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1288078760/genil-river-valley-loop-from-corcoya" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Genil River Valley Loop from Corcoya&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 9.8-mile route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24519540" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Jesus Nazareno – Castillo Anzur loop from Puente Genil&lt;/a&gt; is an even longer, difficult 16.6-mile trail.

Q: What do other runners say about the routes in Badolatosa?
A: The running routes in Badolatosa are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 150 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the scenic river views and the mix of natural and urban environments.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the area?
A: Yes, you can find several circular running routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1288078760/genil-river-valley-loop-from-corcoya" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Genil River Valley Loop from Corcoya&lt;/a&gt; is a notable example, offering a challenging loop through the valley. Many routes are designed to bring you back to your starting point, making them convenient for exploration.

Q: What points of interest can I see while jogging around Badolatosa?
A: Many routes offer scenic views and pass by interesting landmarks. You can experience the natural beauty of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5392067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Genil River Meander&lt;/a&gt;, or pass by historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5392069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of Fuensanta&lt;/a&gt;. Other highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5394288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Malpasillo Dam and Genil River&lt;/a&gt;, and the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6039106" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Ratosa Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the running routes in Badolatosa suitable for families?
A: Many of the easier, flatter routes along the Genil River are suitable for families, especially those with older children. These paths offer a pleasant environment for a family run or walk. Always check the route's difficulty and length to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most outdoor trails in the Badolatosa region, especially on the riverside paths and natural trails. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash and ensure you clean up after them to maintain the natural environment for everyone.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Badolatosa?
A: The best time of year for jogging in Badolatosa is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, sometimes wetter, conditions of winter. This allows for more comfortable and enjoyable runs.

Q: Are there any long-distance running paths available?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer runs, Badolatosa and its surroundings offer several extended paths. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24519540" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Jesus Nazareno – Castillo Anzur loop from Puente Genil&lt;/a&gt; is a significant 16.6-mile route, providing a substantial challenge for long-distance runners. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1287253328/genil-river-meander-and-fuensanta-hermitage-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Genil River Meander and Fuensanta Hermitage Trail&lt;/a&gt; is also a good option at 8.7 miles.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by historical sites?
A: Yes, the region blends natural beauty with historical elements. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1287253328/genil-river-meander-and-fuensanta-hermitage-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Genil River Meander and Fuensanta Hermitage Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, leads you past the significant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5392069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of Fuensanta&lt;/a&gt;, offering a cultural touch to your run.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or cafes along the routes?
A: While many routes follow natural riverbanks, those that pass through or near urban sections, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001013710/puente-genil-run-through-the-urban-area-and-the-genil-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Genil - run through the urban area and the Genil river&lt;/a&gt;, will offer opportunities to find cafes or shops for refreshments. For routes further afield, it's advisable to carry your own water and snacks.
tips: In one of the gorges in this area of the Embalse de Malpasillo Natural Park we find views of Cerro del Pinar, on the other side of the Río Genil.
Beautiful valley through which the Río Genil runs and which offers a wide view of this area that is above the arrival of the reservoir.

faqs.question: Is the Valle del Río Genil — circular desde Corcoya trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this is considered a difficult run. It's best suited for those with very good physical condition due to its length (15.8 km) and significant elevation gain (323 meters). It's not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this jogging route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. You'll find a majority of paved tracks, but also approximately 5 kilometers of unpaved track and 1.46 kilometers of asphalt. This mix means you'll encounter different surfaces, requiring careful footing in some sections.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers picturesque natural surroundings, running along the banks of the Genil River. You can expect to see characteristic riparian vegetation like poplars, willows, and oleanders, and delve into riverine forests. There are also Views of Cerro del Pinar and the Genil River Valley itself.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular jogging route?
faqs.answer: On average, this 15.8-kilometer jogging route takes approximately 2 hours to complete. However, your actual time may vary depending on your pace and fitness level.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: While specific details for this route are not provided, trails along the Río Genil are generally enjoyable in spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring cooler, potentially wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Is the Valle del Río Genil — circular desde Corcoya trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding specific dog rules for this exact trail is not available. However, when jogging with dogs in natural areas, it's generally advisable to keep them on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety, especially on mixed terrain.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee to use this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that a permit or entrance fee is required to jog the Valle del Río Genil — circular desde Corcoya trail.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting and ending in Corcoya. While specific parking details are not provided, you would typically look for parking within the village of Corcoya to access the trailhead.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, approximately 5% of this route also passes through sections of the Vereda de Badolatosa.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for this difficult jogging trail?
faqs.answer: Given its difficult classification and mixed terrain, you should wear appropriate jogging footwear suitable for both paved and unpaved surfaces. Bring enough water, especially during warmer months, and consider carrying a small first-aid kit. Layered clothing is also advisable.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trailhead in Corcoya?
faqs.answer: While specific amenities directly at the trailhead are not detailed, Corcoya is a small village. You might find basic services or local establishments within the village itself, but it's always a good idea to check local listings beforehand if you require specific amenities like cafes or accommodation.
